briefing.summary: As of 2026-03-02, 6 article(s) from The Verge, TechCrunch, Mobile World Live, RCR Wireless News, Qualcomm Newsroom cover growth areas: "Qualcomm's new Snapdragon Wear Elite chip is geared toward wearable AI gadgets"; "Qualcomm wants to be the chip inside whatever replaces your smartphone"; "Snap snaps up Snapdragon XR for eyewear push in multi-year deal"; "Qualcomm is taking a full-stack approach to edge AI"; "Qualcomm IE-IoT Expansion Is Complete: Edge AI Unleashed for Developers, Enterprises and OEMs"; "Qualcomm expects AI-boosting 6G devices in 2029".

articles.title: Qualcomm's new Snapdragon Wear Elite chip is geared toward wearable AI gadgets
articles.description: Qualcomm announced Snapdragon Wear Elite, a "wrist plus" chip on 3nm process with integrated eNPU and Hexagon NPU handling 2B parameters on-device. Supports satellite connectivity, 5G, UWB and Bluetooth 6.0. Designed for AI pendants, pins and display-free smart glasses beyond traditional smartwatches. Linux support added for startup flexibility.

articles.title: Qualcomm wants to be the chip inside whatever replaces your smartphone
articles.description: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon revealed 40+ AI wearable designs in development. Announced Snapdragon Reality Elite XR platform (60% GPU boost, 160% NPU boost) and START toolkit for smart glasses. XREAL Project Aura will be first to market.

articles.title: Snap snaps up Snapdragon XR for eyewear push in multi-year deal
articles.description: Snap subsidiary Specs struck a multi-year agreement with Qualcomm to use Snapdragon XR platform in smart glasses. The deal positions Qualcomm as the primary silicon provider for Snap's next-generation AR devices.

articles.title: Qualcomm is taking a full-stack approach to edge AI
articles.description: Qualcomm built a full-stack edge AI platform through acquisitions of Foundries.io (Linux IoT), Edge Impulse (AI/ML development with 170K+ developers), Advantech partnership (industrial edge) and Arduino (open-source hardware/software). Strategy fuses hardware, software and community from hobbyists to enterprises.

articles.title: Qualcomm IE-IoT Expansion Is Complete: Edge AI Unleashed for Developers, Enterprises and OEMs
articles.description: Qualcomm completed its acquisition of Augentix, a leader in mass-market image processors, extending its ability to provide SoCs for smart cameras and industrial IoT. Combined with Edge Impulse and Foundries.io, this completes Qualcomm's end-to-end vision for security-focused, power-efficient edge AI.

articles.title: Qualcomm expects AI-boosting 6G devices in 2029
articles.description: At MWC Shanghai 2026, Qualcomm SVP John Smee said pre-commercial 6G devices could arrive by 2028 with commercial shipments in 2029. Positioned 6G as the first native AI generation of wireless, with context-aware AI agents merging digital and physical worlds.
